label: Czech Republic / Czech
ladderNote: Everyday Czech from iDNES, Mojepsychologie, and Emimino. Situationship appears in Czech outlets for locals, often glossed as skorovztah.

levels.meaning: Nothing going on.
There's chemistry / a spark. Used in Czech dating/TV speech.
Flirting / actively pursuing. Dictionary-attested colloquial Czech.
We're texting; early talking stage.
Getting to know each other.
We kissed / made out.
One-night stand / one-off hookup. Used on Czech forums and podcasts.

levels.term: Kamarádi s benefity / přátelství s výhodami
levels.meaning: Friends with benefits; common Czech calque, covered in Czech psychology/lifestyle media.
We're meeting up / seeing each other casually.
We're dating / going on dates.
Undefined ongoing thing. Czech sites often say skorovztah; situationship is the English loan used in Czech media.
We're officially a couple; the classic Czech label (iDNES etc.).
We're together / we're a pair; solid couple status.
Declared love to each other—the natural concise Czech expression for this mutual milestone.
My partner; more adult than přítel/přítelkyně.
We live together.
Engaged.
Mother/father of my child—the ordinary Czech descriptive equivalent; there is no established matching slang label.
Married.
Married with kids.
